items.final_wording: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)
source.population: Primarily adults and adolescents with tinea pedis; the guideline explicitly does not cover all dermatophyte subtypes.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth tinea-pedis documentation; only population- and site-compatible fields are mapped from the broader fungal workflow.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)
source.population: Primarily adults and adolescents with tinea pedis; the guideline explicitly does not cover all dermatophyte subtypes.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.

items.rationale: The exact UAE DHA section supports documenting a tinea-pedis review, interval course, itch, scale, fissures, maceration, redness, vesicles, ulcers, foot distribution, clinician inspection and impact without inferring active fungal infection.

items.final_wording: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)
source.population: Primarily adults and adolescents with tinea pedis; the guideline explicitly does not cover all dermatophyte subtypes.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth tinea-pedis documentation; only population- and site-compatible fields are mapped from the broader fungal workflow.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.

items.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports documenting severe pain, diabetes, immunosuppression, spread, secondary infection and systemic illness only when assessed.

items.final_wording: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

items.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording laboratory results already available without generating microscopy, culture or biopsy.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)
source.population: Primarily adults and adolescents with tinea pedis; the guideline explicitly does not cover all dermatophyte subtypes.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth tinea-pedis documentation; only population- and site-compatible fields are mapped from the broader fungal workflow.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.

added_items.rationale: The exact investigation section supports recording laboratory results already available without generating microscopy, culture or biopsy.
